task_completion.description: Open the properties for the 'CryptoCore' project.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Visual Studio shows the project properties tab for 'CryptoCore' as the active document.

task_completion.description: Enable the setting to sign the assembly.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The 'Sign the assembly' checkbox is checked in the Signing or Build > Strong naming section of the project properties.

task_completion.description: Create a new strong name key file named 'company_key.snk'.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The 'Create Strong Name Key' dialog is visible with 'company_key.snk' entered as the Key file name, or 'company_key.snk' is actively selected in the strong name key file dropdown.

task_completion.description: Ensure the new key file is not password protected.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The 'Protect my key file with a password' checkbox is unchecked in the 'Create Strong Name Key' dialog.

task_completion.description: Rebuild the solution successfully to produce the signed assembly.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The Visual Studio Output window shows a successful build or rebuild (e.g., 'Rebuild All: 1 succeeded', 'Build succeeded') occurring after the signing configuration.

integrity.description: Agent used the Visual Studio IDE to configure signing and rebuild.
integrity.visual_evidence: All actions, including property changes and building, occur within the Visual Studio 2022 IDE interface, rather than using an external command prompt or standalone text editor.

integrity.description: Build output is genuine and generated by the IDE.
integrity.visual_evidence: The Output window displays standard MSBuild/Visual Studio build logs with realistic build steps, not manually typed text or pasted strings.

integrity.description: The key file was genuinely created and added to the project.
integrity.visual_evidence: The Solution Explorer shows 'company_key.snk' as a file under the CryptoCore project, confirming it was actually created.
